channel.title: FreshRaise - Weekly funded-startup signals
channel.description: Each week: freshly-funded startups, the role to contact, and a ready-to-send angle.

item.title: FreshRaise 2026-W26: 25 freshly-funded accounts to sell to this week
item.description:
FreshRaise Issue 2026-W26

Freshly-funded B2B SaaS & tech startups (pre-seed -> Series B)

25 trigger-ready accounts, scored and contextualized.

Almetra (formerly Deltia)

heat 88

Trigger: €16.3M Series A led by blisce/, with NAP, Merantix Capital, Robert Bosch Venture Capital (Tech.eu & EU-Startups, as of 25 Jun 2026)

Why now: Two-source corroborated Series A explicitly earmarked for product development, US expansion and platform build-out — a textbook moment for growth, hiring and go-to-market services. Berlin-based manufacturing intelligence with Bosch/Siemens Energy ties means real budget.

Who to contact: VP Marketing / Head of Growth (or COO for US expansion)

Angle: Reference their US market entry: 'You just raised €16.3M to cross the Atlantic — here's how we'd stand up your US demand-gen engine before the first sales hires land.'

source

Compri

heat 82

Trigger: €3.2M seed led by Picus Capital (Tech.eu, as of 24 Jun 2026)

Why now: Milan-based AI procurement SaaS just closed seed capital and is at the team-building stage — prime for RevOps, growth marketing and recruiting partners. Fresh signal, on-vertical B2B SaaS.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO or Head of Growth

Angle: 'Picus-backed seed means you're about to hire your first GTM team — want a RevOps stack blueprint that won't need rebuilding at Series A?'

source

Kalipso

heat 80

Trigger: $3.2M funding round (Tech.eu, as of 25 Jun 2026)

Why now: Barcelona RegTech SaaS just raised — compliance platforms scaling out of seed need content/PR and demand-gen to win enterprise trust. Fresh and on-vertical.

Who to contact: CEO or Head of Marketing

Angle: 'Lawyers+engineers built the product — but enterprise buyers need trust signals. Let's build the case-study and PR engine that turns your raise into pipeline.'

source

HR Duo

heat 80

Trigger: €1.6M led by Puma Growth Partners, with Enterprise Ireland (EU-Startups, as of 25 Jun 2026)

Why now: Irish HRTech SaaS raised specifically to grow its UK sales team and strengthen SaaS infrastructure — explicit hiring and dev/RevOps demand named in the announcement.

Who to contact: Head of Sales / Head of Revenue

Angle: 'You said the raise is for building the UK sales team — want a recruiting + RevOps onboarding playbook so your new reps ramp in weeks, not quarters?'

source

SE3 Labs

heat 78

Trigger: Emerged from stealth backed by Lakestar, Seedcamp and EWOR (Tech.eu, as of 26 Jun 2026)

Why now: Spatial AI deeptech just unveiled with top-tier backers — stealth exit means new brand, website, positioning and first GTM hires all in play right now.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO or Head of Marketing

Angle: 'Just out of stealth with Lakestar+Seedcamp behind you — first impressions matter. Want a launch-week positioning and PR sprint to capture the attention window?'

source

Serpier

heat 76

Trigger: €1.4M from True Collective and EIFO (Tech.eu, as of 25 Jun 2026)

Why now: Danish martech SaaS for e-commerce visibility just raised — they sell marketing tech but still need their own growth, design and dev capacity to scale. Adjacent peers make natural partners too.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO or Head of Product

Angle: 'You help retailers get found — we can help you get your own platform to scale-ready before your next raise. Spare 15 min?'

source

XCures

heat 75

Trigger: $46M Series B led by Innovius Capital (Crunchbase, as of 24 Jun 2026)

Why now: AI-for-medical-records SaaS just closed a $46M Series B — top of our vertical range, with budget to scale GTM, RevOps and recruiting fast.

Who to contact: VP Marketing / Head of Revenue

Angle: 'A $46M Series B means aggressive scaling targets — let's pressure-test your RevOps and demand engine before the board's first review.'

source

Fika Jobs

heat 74

Trigger: $4M round for video-first AI hiring platform (TechCrunch, as of 23 Jun 2026)

Why now: HR-tech SaaS just raised $4M to build out a video-first hiring product with AI agents — at this stage they need growth marketing, design and engineering scale.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO or Head of Growth

Angle: 'You're disrupting interviews with AI agents — let's make sure your own demand-gen funnel converts as well as your product does. Quick ideas inside.'

source

Kyrok

heat 70

Trigger: €3.1M pre-seed (FinSMEs / The SaaS News, as of 23 Jun 2026)

Why now: Two-source-corroborated €3.1M pre-seed — a sizeable pre-seed for a SaaS startup that will be hiring and building GTM imminently.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: 'A €3.1M pre-seed is a strong runway — want a 90-day GTM foundation plan so you hit Series A metrics with the cash you just raised?'

source

Wakeline

heat 68

Trigger: €2.1M pre-seed led by TechVision Fonds (Tech.eu, as of 24 Jun 2026)

Why now: Düsseldorf deeptech (continuously adaptive AI) at pre-seed — small but fresh, with founders who'll outsource design/dev/brand before building in-house. Early relationship wins compound.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: 'Pre-seed is the cheapest time to nail your brand and developer-facing docs — want a quick teardown of how to position adaptive-AI to skeptical buyers?'

source

AethexAI

heat 67

Trigger: $3M pre-seed for full-stack voice AI infrastructure (afrotech.com, as of 22 Jun 2026)

Why now: Voice AI infra startup targeting enterprises in emerging markets just raised pre-seed — needs brand, GTM and dev partners to reach enterprise buyers credibly.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: 'Selling voice-AI infra to enterprises means winning procurement and trust — want an enterprise-ready positioning and content plan to match your $3M ambition?'

source

Cambrian

heat 66

Trigger: $6M seed, a16z CSX-backed, blockchain data oracle network (The Block, as of 24 Jun 2026)

Why now: a16z-backed seed for B2B blockchain infra — strong investor signal, building stage, needs dev-rel, content and recruiting partners.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO or Head of DevRel

Angle: 'a16z CSX seed gets you attention — want a developer-relations and content plan to convert that buzz into integrations?'

source

Acodyne

heat 64

Trigger: €2.5M pre-seed for autonomous cargo drones (Resilience Media, as of 25 Jun 2026)

Why now: Copenhagen deeptech just raised a sizeable pre-seed — early hardware/SaaS hybrid that will need branding, recruiting and engineering support to scale.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: 'Cargo drones live or die on regulatory trust and a sharp narrative — want help building the story investors and customers buy into post-pre-seed?'

source

LiveKid

heat 62

Trigger: Acquired Aldea to expand into Latin America (Tech.eu, as of 24 Jun 2026)

Why now: Preschool-management SaaS expanding via acquisition into Mexico/Colombia/Chile — M&A integration and new-market GTM means localization, RevOps and marketing demand.

Who to contact: Head of Growth / COO

Angle: 'Acquiring Aldea is step one — integrating teams and localizing GTM for LatAm is the hard part. Want a 60-day integration and market-entry plan?'

source

Wayout International

heat 60

Trigger: €2.42M Series A extension (Tech.eu, as of 25 Jun 2026)

Why now: Swedish climatetech scaling decentralised water infrastructure — Series A extension means active scaling and likely marketing/recruiting needs. Borderline on-vertical (hardware-heavy) but a SaaS/platform play.

Who to contact: Head of Growth / COO

Angle: 'A Series A extension to scale deployment is the moment to professionalise your B2B marketing — want a plan to turn pilots into a repeatable pipeline?'

source

AppsFlyer

heat 58

Trigger: Reportedly $1B Series E at $2.7B valuation, eyeing IPO (Crunchbase, as of 22 Jun 2026)

Why now: (unverified) Late-stage marketing analytics raise (above Series B target ceiling but adjacent) — IPO-prep means demand for PR, fractional CFO/IR and RevOps. Treat as edge-of-vertical.

Who to contact: CFO / Head of IR

Angle: 'IPO chatter means your finance and comms functions are about to be under a microscope — want fractional IPO-readiness support to plug the gaps?'

source

Sovra

heat 56

Trigger: $2M pre-seed, stablecoin-powered fintech (EnterpriseAM, as of 22 Jun 2026)

Why now: (unverified) New fintech just raised pre-seed — fintech compliance and brand needs are acute, and early-stage means outsourced services. Single thin source.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: 'Stablecoin fintech at pre-seed needs airtight compliance comms from day one — want a quick audit of how your messaging reads to regulators and partners?'

source

Lytmus AI

heat 54

Trigger: ₹5 crore pre-seed led by Boundless Ventures (Business Standard, as of 28 Jun 2026)

Why now: (unverified) Fresh AI pre-seed in India — early stage SaaS that will outsource growth and dev work. Single source.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: 'Just closed your pre-seed with Boundless — want a lean GTM kit so your first marketing rupee works twice as hard?'

source

ARX Industries (ARX Robotics + Roboneers JV)

heat 54

Trigger: New JV formed to industrialise unmanned ground vehicle production (Tech.eu, as of 25 Jun 2026)

Why now: A newly formed joint venture / expansion event — new entity means new brand, hiring and ops setup. Defence-hardware focus lowers SaaS fit but it's a genuine expansion trigger.

Who to contact: COO / Head of Operations

Angle: 'A brand-new JV needs a brand, careers page and ops backbone fast — want a launch sprint to get ARX Industries market-ready in weeks?'

source

Sherlocks AI

heat 52

Trigger: Rs 7.5 crore pre-seed led by SenseAI Ventures (Indian Startup Times, as of 23 Jun 2026)

Why now: (unverified) AI pre-seed in India — building stage, candidate for outsourced growth/design/dev. Single thin source.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: 'Pre-seed AI startups win or lose on early positioning — want a quick teardown of how to make your product story land with B2B buyers?'

source

Lumenai

heat 50

Trigger: Pre-seed round (The SaaS News, as of 24 Jun 2026)

Why now: (unverified) SaaS pre-seed with no amount disclosed — on-vertical and fresh, worth a light-touch early outreach. Single thin source, amount unknown.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: 'Congrats on the pre-seed — want a no-strings GTM foundation checklist tailored to early SaaS to make sure month one isn't wasted?'

source

Point2 Technology Inc.

heat 48

Trigger: SEC Form D filed (EDGAR, as of 26 Jun 2026)

Why now: (unverified) Fresh Form D filing indicates a new securities offering/raise; tech company name but amount and round not detailed in the filing summary. Worth confirming before heavy outreach.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO or CFO

Angle: 'Saw your new Form D filing — as you put the new capital to work, want a quick read on where outsourced GTM or finance support pays back fastest?'

source

Intu Diagnostics

heat 46

Trigger: €1.1M strategic bridge round; total funding now €3M+ (EU-Startups, as of 26 Jun 2026)

Why now: Leipzig diagnostics platform raised a bridge — proceeds for IP and platform work; smaller/biotech-leaning so lower fit, but a genuine fresh trigger for dev/regulatory/PR services.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: 'Lab-free diagnostics needs a crisp story for partners and regulators — want help sharpening your platform narrative as you put the bridge to work?'

source

Tissium

heat 44

Trigger: €30M equity + up to €30M debt (Tech.eu, as of 24 Jun 2026)

Why now: French medtech raised substantial equity+debt — biotech/medical lowers SaaS fit, but a sizeable, fresh raise with scaling and hiring needs for PR and fractional finance.

Who to contact: CFO / Head of Comms

Angle: 'A €30M equity + €30M debt structure adds finance and IR complexity — want fractional CFO support to keep reporting investor-grade as you scale?'

source

Leyden Labs

heat 42

Trigger: €40M raise; EIC Fund, Invest-NL, Gates Foundation, ClavystBio (EU-Startups, as of 25 Jun 2026)

Why now: Dutch biotech raised a large round — off-core for SaaS service sellers but a genuine large funding trigger; relevant for PR, recruiting and fractional finance providers willing to work biotech.

Who to contact: Head of People / Head of Comms

Angle: 'A €40M raise with the Gates Foundation in the syndicate raises your public profile overnight — want a comms and recruiting plan to match the attention?'

source

(c) FreshRaise. For subscriber use only. Signals sourced from public news; verify before outreach.
